name: SoftBound + CETS — Software Pointer-Bounds Research
summary: Paired research project from UPenn: SoftBound provides pure-software pointer-based spatial bounds via LLVM IR instrumentation with disjoint metadata; CETS adds identifier-based temporal safety. Combined overhead ~116%. Research only; influence visible in HWASan/MTE designs and CHERI's pointer-with-metadata model.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Pure software pointer-based bounds (SoftBound) and identifier-based temporal safety (CETS) via LLVM IR instrumentation with disjoint metadata. Overhead: SoftBound ~83% spatial, +CETS = ~116% combined.

Research only; never deployed in production toolchains. Worth naming for design genealogy: the influence is visible in HWASan/MTE designs (per-pointer metadata for memory safety) and CHERI's pointer-with-metadata model (capabilities are the hardware-enforced version of SoftBound's per-pointer bounds).

sections.title: SoftBound — spatial safety
sections.content:
SoftBound (UPenn) is a compiler-instrumentation pass that maintains, for every pointer in the program, a separate `(base, bound)` metadata pair stored in disjoint shadow tables. Loads and stores check the pointer against its metadata. Spatial overhead ~83%.

The key contribution was the *disjoint metadata* design: the pointer representation itself is unchanged (still a regular C pointer), keeping ABI compatibility, while bounds live in a separate shadow.

sections.title: CETS — temporal safety
sections.content:
CETS (Compiler-Enforced Temporal Safety) extends SoftBound with a per-pointer *identifier* — a unique key — and a per-allocation lock; deallocation invalidates the lock so subsequent uses of the now-stale pointer fail the key/lock check. Combined SoftBound+CETS overhead ~116%.

CETS established the key/lock pattern that recurs in later temporal-safety mechanisms (e.g. CHERI's tagged validity bit, CHERIoT's revocation).
